steps.title: Apply the quotient rule for exponents
steps.content:
When dividing powers with the same base, subtract the exponents. Apply this separately to $x$ and $y$:
$$\frac{x^{-2}}{x^{1/3}} = x^{-2 - \frac{1}{3}} = x^{-\frac{6}{3} - \frac{1}{3}} = x^{-\frac{7}{3}}$$
$$\frac{y^{\frac{1}{2}}}{y^{-1}} = y^{\frac{1}{2} - (-1)} = y^{\frac{1}{2} + 1} = y^{\frac{3}{2}}$$
So the entire expression becomes:
$$x^{-\frac{7}{3}} \cdot y^{\frac{3}{2}} = \frac{y^{\frac{3}{2}}}{x^{\frac{7}{3}}}$$

steps.title: Convert fractional exponents to radical form
steps.content:
Rewrite each fractional exponent as a sum of whole and fractional parts.

Numerator: $y^{\frac{3}{2}} = y^{1+\frac{1}{2}} = y \cdot y^{\frac{1}{2}} = y\sqrt{y}$

Denominator: $x^{\frac{7}{3}} = x^{2+\frac{1}{3}} = x^2 \cdot x^{\frac{1}{3}} = x^2\sqrt[3]{x}$

Therefore:
$$\frac{y\sqrt{y}}{x^2\sqrt[3]{x}}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
